question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() does not archive deleted TopicMessage records before deletion, causing irreversible loss of Hashgraph history with no audit trail, by triggering the retention job at a time when the retention period configuration is temporarily misconfigured?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a minimum retention floor in prune() to cause deletion of TopicMessage records that are still referenced by active GRPC subscriptions, causing in-flight consumers to receive an incomplete or truncated view of the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on RetentionJob.prune() being absent at the repository layer to cause concurrent prune() invocations that delete overlapping ranges of TopicMessage records, resulting in a non-atomic deletion that leaves the runningHash chain in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() does not validate whether the consensusTimestamp parameter corresponds to a real RecordFile boundary, causing deletion of TopicMessage records that span across a record file boundary and breaking the cross-file runningHash chain continuity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the prune() method to be called with a negative consensusTimestamp value by exploiting a long underflow in the retention period calculation, resulting in the JPQL condition 'consensusTimestamp <= negative_value' matching no records but masking a logic error that could later be triggered to delete all records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a foreign key constraint check in prune() to delete TopicMessage records that are still referenced by topic_message_lookup entries, causing the lookup index to point to non-existent history and enabling history falsification through stale index entries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicMessageLookupRepository.prune() and TopicMessageRepository.prune() are called in separate transactions within the RetentionJob, causing a window where topic_message records are deleted but topic_message_lookup entries still exist, enabling an attacker to query stale lookup data that references deleted Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a topicId that references a deleted or non-existent topic entity, and since TopicMessageRepository.save() does not validate topicId referential integrity, cause a TopicMessage record with an orphaned topicId to be stored, corrupting the topic-level Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a sequenceNumber of Long.MAX_VALUE (9223372036854775807), and since TopicMessageRepository.save() does not validate sequenceNumber bounds, cause a sequence number overflow that corrupts the monotonic ordering invariant of the Hashgraph topic message history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a chunkTotal of 0 or a negative value, and since TopicMessageRepository.save() does not validate chunkTotal > 0, cause a TopicMessage record with an invalid chunkTotal to be stored, breaking the chunked message reassembly logic and corrupting multi-part Hashgraph message history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a chunkNum equal to chunkTotal but with a different initialTransactionId than the first chunk, and since TopicMessageRepository.save() does not validate initialTransactionId consistency across chunks, cause a multi-part message to be stored with inconsistent chunk metadata, tampering with the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @JsonTypeInfo and @JsonTypeName annotations on TopicMessage to inject a crafted JSON payload through a deserialization endpoint that creates a TopicMessage with arbitrary field values, bypassing the normal importer validation pipeline and directly corrupting the Hashgraph history stored via TopicMessageRepository.save()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ToString.Exclude annotation on the message and runningHash fields to cause logging systems to omit these fields from audit logs, enabling silent tampering of these critical Hashgraph history fields without detection in log-based monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() JPQL query uses a JPQL entity name 'TopicMessage' rather than a native table name, causing the query to be affected by Hibernate entity mapping changes that could alter which records are deleted, potentially erasing more Hashgraph history than intended?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a pre-prune snapshot or backup mechanism in TopicMessageRepository.prune() to cause irreversible deletion of Hashgraph history by triggering the retention job during a network partition, where the deletion succeeds locally but the distributed consensus state is not yet fully replicated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of row-level locking in the prune() DELETE query to cause a phantom read scenario where new TopicMessage records inserted between the retention boundary calculation and the DELETE execution are incorrectly included in the deletion, erasing freshly imported Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() returns an int count of deleted rows without any upper-bound validation, causing a scenario where an unexpectedly large number of TopicMessage records are deleted in a single call without triggering any alert or rollback, permanently destroying Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum-records-retained check in prune() to cause deletion of all TopicMessage records for a topic, including the genesis message with sequenceNumber=1, permanently destroying the anchor point of the Hashgraph running hash chain for that topic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() does not differentiate between topics when deleting records, causing a scenario where a high-frequency topic's messages push the retention boundary forward, inadvertently triggering deletion of a low-frequency topic's entire history that falls within the same timestamp range?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a runningHash of exactly 48 bytes (SHA-384 length) but containing all zeros, and since TopicMessageRepository.save() does not validate that the runningHash is a valid SHA-384 digest of the previous state, cause a falsified runningHash to be permanently stored in the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdConverter used for topicId to submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a topicId that encodes to a different entity than intended (e.g., through shard/realm/num encoding ambiguity), causing the TopicMessage to be stored under the wrong topicId and corrupting the Hashgraph history for both the intended and unintended topics?
